title: camelina edentula
intro: Camelina edentula, also known as false flax, is a flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a non-edible plant that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of camelina edentula include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by infusing its flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using its dried flowers in capsules or tinctures.
